title: Auto-file new dev issues under tracking issues
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
The project-manager automation added new issues to the Dev board but left them unparented, so they never joined a tracking issue. This wires that up.

- Fetches open `DEV-TRACKING` issues and lets the classifier pick the best-fit parent, then links the new issue as a native sub-issue
- Routes quest-labelled issues (`POLLEN-QUEST`/`DRAFT-QUEST`) to Dev with a `DEV-*` label instead of skipping them
- Adds `DEV-APP` and `DEV-UI-UX` to the valid dev labels + prompt (previously filtered out)
- Refreshes deprecated tier names in the prompt

Guards: skips PRs and tracking issues themselves, and only links to a number actually present in the live list.

gist.headline: The hive mind gets organized
gist.blurb: Our GitHub project manager bot just got smarter. New dev issues are now automatically sorted into their proper tracking epics, keeping the hive perfectly organized.
gist.summary: Updated the project-manager GitHub automation to automatically link new dev issues as sub-issues under the best-fit DEV-TRACKING epic, while also routing quest-labelled issues to the Dev board.
gist.impact: Contributors will see new issues automatically categorized and parented to tracking epics, reducing manual triage work.
gist.visual_concept: The robot monitor character and Polly the bee organizing files or folders. The robot is sorting papers into neat stacks or filing cabinets.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of a cute chunky robot with a CRT monitor head organizing glowing digital folders into a neat filing cabinet. The tiny bee mascot hovers nearby holding a small clipboard, checking off tasks. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
